rows.notes: Values taken from gemini-flash (row 1), which accurately read the pounds, empty shillings (represented by a dash), and pence. The description was combined from the image's top-right 'Bl 300-' and the main text 'Of Bursar pro Bibled'. gpt-5-mini (row 1) misread shillings as '4' and missed pence '11', only extracting the main text part of the description.

rows.description: Reimbursement to Domus per Guineas Due
rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across both extractors (gemini-flash row 2, gpt-5-mini row 2) and match the image. The description was transcribed directly from the image, partially matching gpt-5-mini's start but correcting its misreading of 'Quinquartation' to 'Guineas Due'. gemini-flash incorrectly assigned the description of the previous row to this entry.
rows.english_description: Payment reimbursing the college household (Domus) for guineas due.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment reimbursing the college household (Domus) for guineas due, amounting to £15 18s 10d.

rows.notes: Amounts were taken from gemini-flash (row 3), which correctly identified them for this visual row. The description was taken from gpt-5-mini (row 3), which correctly identified the text for this visual row. Both extractors suffered from severe description-to-amount mapping errors for this and surrounding rows.
rows.english_description: Payment made by the Bursar as an increment to the college household (Domus).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made by the Bursar as an increment to the college household (Domus), amounting to £88 3s 6d.

rows.notes: Description and amounts were taken from gemini-flash (row 5), which correctly identified this entry based on its visual position. gpt-5-mini did not correctly identify this specific entry; its 'Do' entry (gpt-5-mini row 4) had a different description ('Do for B. & Cat.') and incorrect amounts (£61 s7 d3), appearing to be an invented or heavily misinterpreted row.
rows.english_description: Payment by the Bursar for the outstanding balance for Bibles.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment by the Bursar for the outstanding balance for Bibles, amounting to £5 7s 3d.

rows.description: Philibert for furniture in Mr Mathews
rows.notes: Description transcribed directly from the image. Amounts £1057, s9, and pence 0.5 are clearly visible from the image ('1057 / 9½'). Both extractors struggled significantly with this entry: gemini-flash (row 4) had £1057, s9 but the wrong description and pence (d2 instead of d0.5), and then gemini-flash (row 6) used the correct description but for the sub-entry's amount. gpt-5-mini (row 3) had the amount values but for a different description and misread shillings as s7 instead of s9.
rows.english_description: Payment to Philibert for furniture supplied for Mr Matthews' rooms.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Philibert for furniture supplied for Mr Matthews' rooms, amounting to £1057 9s 0.5d.

rows.notes: Description transcribed directly from the image for this indented sub-entry. The amount '16' is visible. gemini-flash (row 6) correctly identified it as 16 shillings, providing an excellent note explaining this inference due to '16 pence' being an invalid value and common ledger misalignments. gpt-5-mini (row 5) misread it as 16 pounds and had a very poor transcription of the description ('pl. Herbert for learning in Mr Matthews Room (Comp Soli Variis)'). gemini-flash's reasoning for shillings is superior.
rows.english_description: Charge or payment relating to a room described as 'Co. F. Soliciting' (abbreviation unclear).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Charge or payment relating to a room described as 'Co. F. Soliciting' (abbreviation unclear), amounting to 16s.

rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across extractors (gemini-flash row 7, gpt-5-mini row 6) and match the image. The description was transcribed from the image; gpt-5-mini's transcription ('D. Hor hedges Benefaction to Alms Hall') was a plausible attempt but misread 'Horridge's' and 'Xmas 1841'. gemini-flash had a completely wrong description (from a previous row).
rows.english_description: Receipt of Dr Horridge's benefaction credited up to Christmas 1841.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt of Dr Horridge's benefaction credited up to Christmas 1841, amounting to £220 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across extractors (gemini-flash row 8, gpt-5-mini row 7) and match the image. The description was transcribed from the image; gpt-5-mini's 'Int on Shuttles deposit' was a plausible but incorrect transcription. gemini-flash had a description from a previous row.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for salt placed into the Stubbs deposit, amounting to £5 18s 8d.

rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across extractors (gemini-flash row 9, gpt-5-mini row 8) and match the image. gpt-5-mini's description is accurate. gemini-flash had a description from a previous row.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of the Rector's prize for the year 1842, amounting to £10 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across extractors (gemini-flash row 10, gpt-5-mini row 9) and match the image. gpt-5-mini's description is accurate. gemini-flash had a description from a previous row.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of the Rector's share of gas charges, amounting to £10 11s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts are consistent across extractors (gemini-flash row 11, gpt-5-mini row 10) and match the image. gpt-5-mini's description is accurate. gemini-flash had a description from a previous row and then an invented 'Deans fees' entry (its row 12) with no amounts, which was erroneous.

rows.description:
Reimbursement to Domus for repairs of St. Nichs
19 March 1841
rows.notes: gemini-flash (row 13) provided a perfect description and amounts, including the correct multi-line description format for the date. gpt-5-mini (row 11) had correct amounts but a less accurate transcription of the date ('(yr to March 1841)').
rows.english_description: Reimbursement to the college household (Domus) for repairs to St Nicholas (dated 19 March 1841).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Reimbursement to the college household (Domus) for repairs to St Nicholas (dated 19 March 1841), amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus across both extractors (gemini-flash row 14, gpt-5-mini row 12) for all values and the half-penny fraction. Confirmed by clear visual verification of the image.
